subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the story in depth, including plot, character traits, and themes
Analyze Lencho’s personality and his faith in God
Examine the role of human kindness and social values
Develop summarizing, inferencing, and critical thinking skills
Relate the story to real-life situations and moral lessons

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Setting of the Story
topics_covered: Author: Gregorio López y Fuentes
Setting: Mexican village, Lencho’s house and farm
Overview of Lencho and his family
teaching_strategy: Ask questions to activate prior knowledge
student_activities: Discuss what kind of life farmers might have
assessment: Oral questions on setting and characters

lesson_plan.title: Lencho and His Farm: Problem and Hope
topics_covered: Lencho’s farm and crops
Drought and its impact on his livelihood
Lencho’s hope for rain
teaching_strategy: Use visual images of drought and rain
student_activities: Draw a simple diagram showing effects of drought and rain
Discuss how they would react in Lencho’s place
assessment: Oral Q&A on why rain was important

lesson_plan.title: Lencho Writes a Letter
topics_covered: Lencho’s faith and trust in God
Writing the letter asking for 100 pesos
Character analysis: hope, simplicity, determination
teaching_strategy: Explain vocabulary and expressions in context
student_activities: Write a few lines as Lencho expressing hope
Highlight words showing faith and determination
assessment: Ask students to summarize Lencho’s request
Short written task: describe Lencho’s traits

lesson_plan.title: Response from the Post Office
topics_covered: Post office employees’ empathy and generosity
Partial fulfillment of Lencho’s request
Human kindness vs. faith in God
teaching_strategy: Storytelling to explain post office incident
student_activities: Role play the post office employees sending money
assessment: Oral questions on post office’s role
Explain why Lencho received only part of the money

lesson_plan.title: Lencho’s Reaction to the Money
topics_covered: Lencho’s reaction to receiving the money
Suspicion toward the post office employees
Faith and trust in God despite doubts
teaching_strategy: Ask students to consider his point of view
student_activities: Debate: Was Lencho right to blame the employees?
Write a short paragraph on faith vs. suspicion
assessment: Class quiz on story events and reactions

lesson_plan.title: Themes and Moral Lessons
topics_covered: Faith, hope, and perseverance
Human kindness and generosity
Simplicity and innocence in characters
teaching_strategy: Use chart to link events to moral lessons
student_activities: List lessons learned from Lencho’s story
Discuss how these values are relevant today
assessment: Short written task on moral lesson
Identify examples of hope and kindness in story

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Chapter Discussion
topics_covered: Summary of the story
Character review: Lencho, family, post office employees
Overall lessons from the story
teaching_strategy: Recap using a timeline and chart of events
Encourage students to explain in their own words
student_activities: Create a timeline of events in the story
Group quiz on characters, events, and lessons
assessment: Oral questioning on characters and events
Class discussion on lessons learned and relevance

teaching_aids: Flashcards of characters and key events
teacher_tip: Focus on character analysis, cause-effect relationships, and moral lessons. Encourage students to express opinions and relate the story to their own experiences.
